name: Proto-Semitic
notes: Proto-Semitic (~5000–4000 BCE). Reconstructed ancestor of all Semitic languages. 29-consonant inventory including: (1) emphatic series /sˤ tˤ ðˤ ɬˤ/; (2) lateral fricatives /ɬ/ (*ś) and /ɬˤ/ (*ṣ́); (3) interdentals /θ ð ðˤ/; (4) pharyngeals /ħ ʕ/; (5) uvular /q/; (6) bilabial stop /p/ (→ f in Arabic). Three-vowel system (a, i, u) with contrastive length. Notation follows Huehnergard (2019) and Lipiński (2001). Script: romanization (PSem was unwritten).
